title: AI Use in Cybersecurity Jumped From 50% to 78% in a Year. AI-Related Failures Rose Sharply Too. New SANS Institute Survey Reveals a Governance Gap.
author: Matt Bromiley
description: The 2026 SANS Institute survey reveals rapid AI adoption in cybersecurity (50% to 78% in one year), but governance and workforce structures have not kept pace. While 78% of organizations report AI-enabled attacks and red teaming adoption jumped to 61%, 63% of practitioners report significant AI shortcomings in threat detection. The report highlights a critical gap between deployment speed and proper oversight, with human expertise remaining essential for effective defense.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Survey sponsor; involvement in cybersecurity solutions market during period of rapid AI adoption and increased security spending.
Survey sponsor; positioned to benefit from increased AI adoption in cybersecurity and growing demand for security solutions.
Survey sponsor; benefits from enterprise AI adoption in security and cloud infrastructure investments.

title: Fortinet to Announce Second Quarter 2026 Financial Results
author: Not Specified
description: Fortinet announced it will hold a conference call on July 29, 2026, at 1:30 p.m. Pacific Time to discuss its second quarter 2026 financial results. The call will be broadcast live on the company's investor relations website, with prepared remarks, supplemental slides, and a replay available afterward.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article is a routine announcement of an earnings conference call with no forward-looking statements, performance metrics, or strategic developments disclosed. It is a standard procedural announcement without positive or negative indicators.

title: Is Cloudflare Overvalued?
author: Marc Guberti
description: Cloudflare's underperformance relative to competitors CrowdStrike and Fortinet is justified due to persistent profitability concerns. Despite solid 34% revenue growth and a strong customer base, the company posted a net operating loss of $62 million in Q1. Trading at over 33x sales with decelerating guidance and no GAAP profitability outlook, Cloudflare leaves little room for error and may not justify its valuation compared to profitable competitors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company remains unprofitable with $62M operating loss, trades at 33x sales with limited valuation flexibility, and guidance suggests revenue growth deceleration. Despite strong customer retention and 34% YoY growth, profitability concerns and high valuation relative to profitable competitors make it an unattractive investment.

Profitable competitor that has outperformed Cloudflare with 49% YoY gains. Profitability and similar valuation multiples to Cloudflare make it a more attractive investment option in the cybersecurity sector.

Best-performing cybersecurity stock mentioned with 87% YoY gains. Profitable company trading at less than half Cloudflare's P/S ratio, making it a more attractive valuation for investors seeking exposure to cybersecurity growth.

title: Fortinet's 74% Surge Since April Is Attracting Attention, but Is It Sustainable?
author: Marc Guberti
description: Fortinet has surged 74% since April, driven by accelerating revenue growth (20% YoY in Q1) and strong tailwinds from AI infrastructure expansion. The cybersecurity company is benefiting from growing attack surfaces created by AI adoption, partnerships with AI leaders, and its unique custom security chips. With over $700 billion expected in big tech AI spending and 800+ AI data centers under construction, Fortinet appears positioned for sustained growth outpacing the broader cybersecurity industry.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong revenue acceleration (20% YoY in Q1), beat guidance expectations, expanding partnerships with AI leaders (Anthropic, OpenAI), unique competitive advantage with custom security chips, and positioned to benefit from massive AI infrastructure buildout with 800+ data centers under construction and $700B+ in expected tech spending.
Mentioned as a competitor that previously had stronger growth reputation than Fortinet, but is now being outpaced by Fortinet year-to-date. No specific negative or positive developments mentioned.
Mentioned as a competitor that previously had stronger growth reputation than Fortinet, but is now being outpaced by Fortinet year-to-date. No specific negative or positive developments mentioned.

Benefits from strong AI chip demand with 85% YoY revenue growth. Fortinet is positioned as one of Nvidia's strategic partners, and increased AI processor deployments will drive demand for Fortinet's security solutions protecting those resources.

title: As AI Data Breaches Become More Common, This Cybersecurity ETF Is Surging
author: Jessica Mitacek
description: Rising AI-enabled cyberattacks are driving unprecedented demand for cybersecurity solutions. The global cybersecurity market is projected to grow from $272 billion in 2025 to $663 billion by 2033 (11.9% CAGR). The Amplify Cybersecurity ETF (HACK) has surged 49% since February 2026, benefiting from increased enterprise security spending as organizations face more sophisticated threats.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: ETF has surged 49% YTD from February low, hitting all-time highs. Receives Moderate Buy rating from analysts covering 96% of portfolio companies. Strong institutional inflows ($171M vs $75M outflows over 12 months) and minimal short interest (0.19%) indicate confidence. Benefits from structural growth in cybersecurity market projected at 11.9% CAGR through 2033.

Listed as top-5 holding in HACK portfolio. Benefits from increased enterprise security spending driven by rising AI-enabled cyberattacks and data breaches. Positioned to capitalize on growing cybersecurity market expansion.
Top-5 holding in HACK portfolio. Gains from structural demand increase for cybersecurity hardware and services as organizations combat sophisticated AI-assisted threats.
Top-5 holding in HACK portfolio. Positioned to benefit from enterprise security budget increases and growing adoption of advanced threat protection solutions.
